body.title: Multiple duplicate _STATUS_LABEL dicts drift apart — consolidate to one source
body.description:
The 7-status display vocab is duplicated across at least three independent dicts: lab/claudecode/queue_view.py:_STATUS_LABEL, devices/web_server/server.py:_STATUS_LABEL (the live /queue render path), and ~/bin/uuopentickets + ~/bin/uumytickets STATUS_LABEL. They drift: the akien-not-legacy fix had to be applied 3+ times and STILL half-landed (DS fixed queue_view.py; server.py kept rendering 'Akien (legacy)' until caught by a render-asserting test — see commits 03277859 + b0e74e72). This is the consequence-checking-gap pattern: a label changed in one place, stale in another. Consolidate to a single canonical status-label source the renderers import, so a label change lands everywhere at once.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/queue_view.py, devices/web_server/server.py (both _STATUS_LABEL/_STATUS_ORDER/_STATUS_CLASS), ~/bin/uuopentickets + ~/bin/uumytickets (note: ~/bin is OUTSIDE the repo — no git net, back up before edit; they may need to keep a small local copy if they can't import the package). Candidate home: a single module under lab/claudecode/ or the canonical status model location.
**Design rules:** test-plan; no-sqlite n/a; docs-live-in-code (the why-akien-is-not-legacy comment should live with the canonical dict).
**Scope boundary:** IN - define ONE canonical {status -> label, order, css-class} source; have queue_view.py + server.py import it; add a test asserting all renderers agree on the akien label and that no canonical (non-legacy) status renders '(legacy)'. OUT - changing the status model/vocab itself; rewriting the bin tools if they genuinely can't import the package (document the residual divergence instead).
**Test plan:** a test that imports both render paths and asserts _STATUS_LABEL['akien'] is identical and contains no 'legacy'; full tests/test_web_queue.py green; manual /queue spot-check.
